zh.lifeSummary: Mary Caterine於1755年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯。她和Nicholas Marshall至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1845年在美國俄亥俄州可倫比納亞里斯本享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: Caterine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Cosmo, Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Vittorio.

variants.name: Catucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carlo, Cosmo, Franco, Pietro, Rocco, Sal, Stefano, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form probably of a diminutive of a short form of the personal name   Caterino  .

variants.name: Caterina
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Battista, Stefano.
Italian: from the female personal name Caterina, a popular name in the medieval period, which was borne by numerous early Christian saints. See also Catherine .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moira
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Máire , now used throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.
Pennsylvania was always against slavery, even though the first settlers, including Penn, came with slaves. Slavery was not prominent in the area.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Pennsylvania Historical & Museum Commission- An Act for the Gradual Abolition of Slavery

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.
Historical Boundaries 1810: Adams, Pennsylvania, United States

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Caterine was born in 1755, in Adams, Pennsylvania, United States. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Nicholas Marshall. She died in 1845, in Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, United States, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Caterine est née en 1755, à Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Nicholas Marshall. Elle est décédée en 1845, à Lisbon, Columbiana, Ohio, États-Unis, à l’âge de 90 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Caterineは1755年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズで生まれた。彼女はNicholas Marshallとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1845年にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州コルンビアナリスボンで90歳で死去した。

ko.Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, U: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 버윅 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Caterine(은)는 1755년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Nicholas Marshall(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1845년 미국 오하이오 주 콜럼비아나 리스본에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.
